---
title: "Lesson 4: Transformations of Distributions"
subtitle: "Statistical Estimation for Data Science and AI"
---

## Transformations of Distributions {#sec-transformations-of-distributions}

::: {.column-margin}
{{< video 01_transformations-of-distributions.mp4 >}}

[transcript](01_transformations-of-distributions.en.txt){target="_blank"}
:::

### Handout

[Handout](02_video-slides-for-transformations-of-distributions_module1_lesson3.pdf){target="_blank"}



### Reflection

We want to do transformations of RVs to get new RVs. 

- For PMF the advice is: "just do it".
- For PDF, we can't apply the transformation to the PDF but we can apply the transformation to its CDF and then differentiate to get the PDF of the transformed RV.
    - this means that we get to play with integrals and derivatives which is so much fun.
    - also we will need to make use of some algebra to recover our "canonical" form of the CDF/PDF from the integral
